mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate transmits into a higher equity present value by easing the discount-rate channel. When FEDFUNDS is falling on a 3-month basis, S&P 500 forward returns over the next month should be positive because valuation multiples expand before real activity fully adjusts.
failure_condition: Reject if falling-FEDFUNDS periods do not deliver higher 20-day SPX returns than non-falling periods, or if the sign of the effect is negative in a rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in unemployment signals weakening income growth and lower earnings expectations, which compresses equities over the subsequent quarter. When UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over three months, S&P 500 forward returns should be negative over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment spikes are followed by positive 60-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below zero.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in retail sales together with housing starts indicates broad demand resilience that supports earnings revisions. If RSAFS and HOUST both trend higher over the prior month, the S&P 500 should rise over the next 20 trading days through stronger growth expectations.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST improvement signal fails to outperform either proxy alone, or if the combined signal has negative average 20-day SPX returns over a rolling 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A broad industrial production slowdown signals weaker real activity and deteriorating corporate revenue momentum. When INDPRO is declining month over month, S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days should be negative because earnings expectations reset lower before inflation relief can offset it.
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the effect disappears in a 3-year rolling evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation is elevated and policy remains restrictive, equities should fall because real rates stay tight and valuation multiples compress. If CPIAUCSL is rising while FEDFUNDS is at or above 4.5%, S&P 500 forward returns over the next month should be negative.
failure_condition: Reject if the CPI-plus-restrictive-rate trigger does not produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if its hit rate falls below 40% across any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A housing-starts downturn is an early demand-sensitive leading indicator for weaker cyclical growth and lower equity returns. When HOUST falls for two consecutive monthly readings, the S&P 500 should underperform over the next week as investors reprice growth-sensitive sectors first.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls for 2 consecutive monthly readings
failure_condition: Reject if two-month housing declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in a majority of triggered instances, or if the average forward return is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A labor-market cooling that is mild rather than abrupt supports equities because it reduces inflation pressure without collapsing demand. When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE stays below 4.0%, the S&P 500 should post positive returns over the next 60 trading days as the market prices easier policy ahead.
failure_condition: Reject if mild labor cooling with sub-4% unemployment does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if deeper labor weakness outperforms this regime.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized shortfall in GDP and retail activity signals broad final-demand weakness that should weigh on earnings and risk appetite over the next month. If GDPC1 momentum is negative and RSAFS is also declining, S&P 500 forward returns should be negative because both output and spending are softening together.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous GDP and retail weakness does not predict negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal has no incremental value versus either series alone.
